summary: Mechanical cleanup that changed header-level `inline` functions and inline declarations to `static inline` across math, SysDolphin baselib, fighter, item, and related headers. The PR body indicates this was done with a broad sed-style replacement. No function bodies or behavior changed in the provided diff; the meaningful change is linkage/definition style for inlines placed in headers.
what_changed: Converted plain `inline` function definitions in headers to `static inline`, including `__fpclassifyf`, `__fpclassifyd`, `fabs_inline`, `HSD_AObjGetEndFrame`, many `HSD_JObj*` accessors/mutators, `fabsf_bitwise`, `HSD_ObjAllocUsed`, `HSD_ObjAllocFree`, `HSD_ObjAllocPeak`, object ref-count helpers, fighter helpers, item helpers, and `GetMarioAttr`.

Converted inline prototypes in `include/sysdolphin/baselib/psstructs.h` to `static inline` prototypes, such as `setBlendMode`, `setupChanCtrl`, `psDispSubPoint`, `psDispSub`, `psDispSubAppSRT`, and `psRemoveBillboardCamera`.
Updated an inline example inside a comment in `src/dolphin/gx/GXEnum.h` from `inline void WriteBP` to `static inline void WriteBP`.
No semantic changes are visible in the diff excerpt: each change is the addition of `static` before existing `inline` declarations or definitions.
smart_moves: Applied a consistent linkage rule to header-resident inline functions, reducing the chance that including the same header in multiple translation units emits externally linked duplicate definitions.
Kept the change mechanical and minimal: function names, bodies, assertions, types, and formatting were otherwise preserved, which lowers matching risk.
Included both actual inline definitions and inline declarations/prototypes, making the style consistent across headers rather than fixing only one subset.
decomp_lessons: For functions defined directly in headers, prefer `static inline` over bare `inline` unless external linkage is intentionally required.
A linkage-only cleanup can be valuable in a decomp project because it reduces build/link ambiguity without disturbing generated code patterns inside call sites.
When introducing helper accessors in headers for matching or readability, declare them `static inline` from the start to avoid cross-translation-unit symbol problems.
Mechanical header-wide refactors are safest when they do not alter bodies, names, assert line numbers, or type signatures.
naming_conventions: Preserved existing project naming rather than renaming during linkage cleanup: examples include `HSD_JObjGetScaleX`, `HSD_ObjAllocUsed`, `getFighter`, `GetItemData`, and `GetMarioAttr`.
SysDolphin baselib APIs keep the `HSD_*` prefix and CamelCase-style function names.
Local melee helper names remain mixed-case or lower camel style as previously written, such as `getFtSpecialAttrsD`, `stickGetDir`, and `ftGetKind`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `static inline` for small header helpers so the compiler can inline them at use sites without requiring a globally emitted symbol.
Because only storage/linkage specifiers changed, this PR is a low-risk tactic for preserving matched function bodies while improving header hygiene.
Do not use this PR as evidence for body-level matching tricks; the diff provides no assembly-specific changes beyond inline linkage.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR was merged quickly with one review recorded, but no textual review feedback is available in the excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that add new header-defined helper functions and check whether they use `static inline` consistently.
Search for remaining bare `inline` declarations in `include` and `src` after PR #659.
Investigate whether any `static inline` prototypes in `psstructs.h` correspond to definitions in C files and whether that style caused later cleanup.
